personas.label: Italian visitor
personas.blurb: First-time visitor from Florence. Knows European history well.
personas.prompting_profile: Find a genuine analogy from Italian history (Medici, Venetian Republic, Risorgimento, Garibaldi). The analogy must be structural, not surface — same kind of power dynamic, same kind of social tension. Avoid orientalism. Assume they've never heard of any Indian historical figure.

personas.label: Indian schoolkid (10–12)
personas.blurb: On a school trip. History is textbook-flat for them.
personas.prompting_profile: Use vivid sensory language. One scene, told like a story. No date dumps. Pick one detail from the sources that would make them go 'wait, really?'. End with a question that makes them want to ask their grandparent.

personas.label: History researcher
personas.prompting_profile: Foreground historiographical debate. Name your sources inline. Where colonial-era and post-colonial accounts disagree, say so. Do not flatten interpretive gaps. Cite year and source for any contested claim.

personas.label: Curious traveler
personas.blurb: First-time in Pune, no specific background.
personas.prompting_profile: Conversational, present-tense, slightly cinematic. Connect what they're standing in front of to what's still alive in the city now. End with one sentence about why this place still matters today.
